# UC-22.11.63 · Daily Log Review Workflow — PCI Queue Ticket Closure SLA

## Description

Tracks time from automated PCI log review finding to analyst disposition so periodic log reviews are not only scheduled but completed with auditable closure records.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS, PCI-DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Tag PCI-related correlation searches consistently. (2) Align 24h SLA to policy. (3) Supervisor dashboard for aging. (4) Export weekly metrics for PCI log review evidence.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `` `notable` `` filtered by `rule_name` like `PCI*` or `tag="pci"`; fields `status`, `closed_time`, `_time`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Tag PCI-related correlation searches consistently. (2) Align 24h SLA to policy. (3) Supervisor dashboard for aging. (4) Export weekly metrics for PCI log review evidence.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
`notable` (tag="pci" OR like(rule_name,"%PCI%")) earliest=-7d
| eval age_h=if(isnotnull(closed_time), (closed_time-_time)/3600, (now()-_time)/3600)
| eval breached=if(isnull(closed_time) AND age_h>24,1,0)
| stats sum(breached) as open_breach, count as total by rule_name
| sort - open_breach
```

Understanding this SPL

**Daily Log Review Workflow — PCI Queue Ticket Closure SLA (PCI DSS Req 10.4.1, 10.4.1.1)** — Tracks time from automated PCI log review finding to analyst disposition so periodic log reviews are not only scheduled but completed with auditable closure records.

Documented **Data sources**: `` `notable` `` filtered by `rule_name` like `PCI*` or `tag="pci"`; fields `status`, `closed_time`, `_time`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Invokes macro `notable` — in Search, use the UI or expand to inspect the underlying SPL.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age_h** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **breached** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by rule_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (rule_name, open_breach), column chart (aging buckets), single value (% closed within SLA).

## SPL

```spl
`notable` (tag="pci" OR like(rule_name,"%PCI%")) earliest=-7d
| eval age_h=if(isnotnull(closed_time), (closed_time-_time)/3600, (now()-_time)/3600)
| eval breached=if(isnull(closed_time) AND age_h>24,1,0)
| stats sum(breached) as open_breach, count as total by rule_name
| sort - open_breach
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user, All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (rule_name, open_breach), column chart (aging buckets), single value (% closed within SLA).
